messages.subject: [R-SIG-Mac] Running R in Lima within Mac system
messages.subject_clean: Running R in Lima within Mac system
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

I am using Ubuntu within Mac (Apple M3, OS Sequoia) based on the Lima
distribution. Details here,

https://lima-vm.io/docs/usage

Now within that Ubuntu I installed R based on guidance available in

https://www.digitalocean.com/community/tutorials/how-to-install-r-on-ubuntu-20-04

However whenever I run R, I stagely get any error message stating that

Error in library(dplyr) : there is no package called ?dplyr?

If I try to install dplyr, or any other package, I get error

> install.packages('dplyr')

Installing package into
?/home/aaa.linux/R/aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu-library/4.4?

(as ?lib? is unspecified)

also installing the dependencies ?utf8?, ?pkgconfig?, ?withr?, ?cli?,
?generics?, ?glue?, ?lifecycle?, ?magrittr?, ?pillar?, ?R6?, ?rlang?,
?tibble?, ?tidyselect?, ?vctrs?

messages.subject_clean: Running R in Lima within Mac system
messages.body_plain:
Dear Christofer Bogaso,

> However whenever I run R, I stagely get any error message stating that
> 
> Error in library(dplyr) : there is no package called ?dplyr?

Is there an .Rprofile somewhere with a call to library(dplyr) in it?

-- 
Best regards,
Ivan

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Mac] Arm64 issues?
messages.body_plain:
In trying to update my packages today,  a lot of updates were not doing anything,  and in checking on that the Arm64 binaries for a lot of packages seem to be lagging say the x86_64 versions, which do have binaries (and why my updates weren?t working).  Not a complaint,  just a heads up,  thanks for all the work you do,

-Roy
messages.body_snippet: In trying to update my packages today,  a lot of updates were not doing anything,  and in checking on that the Arm64 binaries for a lot of packages seem to be lagging say the x86_64 versions, which do

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Mac] Arm64 issues?
messages.body_plain:
Roy,

Thank, yes, the arm64 build machine is catching up. It may take a bit since we are building from older to devel now and there is a backlog after the CRAN summer break. There was a problem with the builds where a system service stopped all builds, but that has been restored.

Cheers,
Simon


> On 26/08/2025, at 10:06, Roy Mendelssohn - NOAA Federal via R-SIG-Mac <r-sig-mac at r-project.org> wrote:

> -Roy

>

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Mac] 'cmath' file not found
messages.body_plain:
New to the list.

Got a new laptop, running OS 15.5 Sequoia.
R version 4.5.1 (2025-06-13) -- "Great Square Root"
Have new Xcode, new gofortran

Trying to install an R package that runs C++, have the R dependencies, the error in installation is:

** using staged installation
** libs
using C++ compiler: ?Apple clang version 16.0.0 (clang-1600.0.26.6)?
using SDK: ?MacOSX15.2.sdk?
[??. Removing some non relevant info??]

fatal error: 'cmath' file not found
   37 | #include <cmath>
      |          ^~~~~~~
1 error generated.

Can any one advise? Thank you!



	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Mac] Which version of clang does CRAN's R build use?
messages.subject_clean: Which version of clang does CRAN's R build use?
messages.body_plain:
The instructions on https://mac.r-project.org/tools/ say to install 
tools using `sudo xcode-select --install`.  Currently for me that installed

   Apple clang version 17.0.0 (clang-1700.0.13.5)

Is that the version of clang that is being used in the CRAN builds?  If 
not, is there an advantage to matching what was used?

Duncan Murdoch
messages.body_snippet:

   Apple clang version 17.0.0 (clang-1700.0.13.5)

Is

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Mac] Which version of clang does CRAN's R build use?
messages.subject_clean: Which version of clang does CRAN's R build use?
messages.body_plain:
On 2025-08-31 4:11 p.m., Duncan Murdoch wrote:
> The instructions on https://mac.r-project.org/tools/ say to install
> tools using `sudo xcode-select --install`.  Currently for me that installed
> 
>     Apple clang version 17.0.0 (clang-1700.0.13.5)
> 
> Is that the version of clang that is being used in the CRAN builds?  If
> not, is there an advantage to matching what was used?
> 

Just found on the download page for R 4.5.1 "This release uses Xcode 
16.2(arm64)/14.2(x86_64) and GNU Fortran 14.2."  So that answers my 
first question, but leaves the second one.  Is it worth installing that 
version?

Duncan Murdoch
